_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
reference_document_element_description: Track data compliance remediation activities, including milestone monitoring, blocker escalation, and closure verification with evidence.
comments: DCWF task intersects with WIDAI RC domain. RC-T-005 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: DQ-T-013 (DQ, cosine: 0.371), RC-T-003 (RC, cosine: 0.370).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 4.8/10 (raw 0.475). Shared vocabulary: [remediation]. DCWF-only terms [actions, approve] vs WIDAI-only terms [activities, blocker, closure, compliance, data, escalation] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.888 (P=0.910, R=0.868). P exceeds R by 0.042 — the DCWF element's 3 words are well-captured by the WIDAI KSA's 15 words, but the KSA contains additional specificity not reflected in the DCWF element.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (99.3%). "Approve remediation actions." and "Track data compliance remediation activities, including milestone monitoring, bl" are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.458 ≈ STS 0.475 (gap 0.017). Independent encoding and cross-attention agree. Shared terms [remediation] are sufficient for independent encoding to detect the match.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 5.9%. Shared tokens: [remediation]. DCWF-only: [actions, approve]. WIDAI-only: [activities, blocker, closure, compliance, data, escalation].
